examples: The burst real estate bubble that kicked off this crisis is unlikely to reinflate quickly.
When the bubble predictably bursts, get the Federal Reserve to pour trillions of dollars into the industry in a desperate attempt to reinflate the bubble.
Whether easy money policies can cure unemployment or reinflate the housing market is on our minds a questionable proposition, but something Chairman Bernanke seems to wanting to do.
When bubbles burst they do not reinflate.
